entities.name: Bangkok Chain Hospital Public Company Limited

financial.note: SSO holds ~3.57% stake; the Jan 2026 wage ceiling hike to 17,500 THB increases the per-head contribution base, supporting reimbursement growth for BCH's large SSO patient base.
Provides debt facilities for the Suvarnabhumi and Rayong expansion projects; debt servicing costs are sensitive to floating rate shifts.
Holds ~1.88% stake; price action often moves in tandem due to shared boardroom ties and mid-tier healthcare sector rotation.

supply_chain.note: Primary distributor for pharma and consumables; pricing fluctuations in imported drugs directly affect gross margins.
Supplies technical healthcare products and imaging technology; high sensitivity to THB weakness on import costs.

competitors.note: Direct rival in the suburban SSO and middle-income segments; often trades on identical macro catalysts.
Competes with BCH's World Medical brand for premium international and insurance patients.
Rival for high-end medical tourism; BCH’s 2026 Kuwaiti patient certification creates direct competition for Middle Eastern referrals.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The 2026 move to 17,500 THB is a fundamental catalyst for cash flow growth in the public healthcare segment.
Certification as a preferred provider for Kuwaiti referrals (started March 2026) drives high-margin revenue for the WMC segment.
Weak THB attracts fly-in patients (Cambodia, Laos, Middle East), though it increases medical equipment CAPEX.
Energy costs for 24/7 hospital operations are a significant portion of non-medical OPEX.
